### Events

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mossor%C3%B3&action=edit&section=22 "Edit section: Events")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/d/d4/Chuva_de_Bala_no_pa%C3%ADs_de_Mossor%C3%B3.jpg/250px-Chuva_de_Bala_no_pa%C3%ADs_de_Mossor%C3%B3.jpg)](/wiki/File:Chuva_de_Bala_no_pa%C3%ADs_de_Mossor%C3%B3.jpg)

"Bullet Rain in the Land of Mossoró" performance, held during Mossoró Cidade Junina

The Mossoró Cidade Junina, one of the largest [June festivals](/wiki/Festa_Junina "Festa Junina") in Northeast Brazil, attracting over one million tourists,[[205]](#cite_note-205) has been held since 1996 at the Arts Station throughout June. It features *[quadrilha](/wiki/Quadrille "Quadrille")* performances, musical shows, food stalls with typical dishes, and cultural projects.[[206]](#cite_note-MCJ-206) During the event, the theatrical performance *Bullet Rain in the Land of Mossoró* recounts the story of [Lampião](/wiki/Lampi%C3%A3o "Lampião") and his gang's 1927 invasion, performed since 2003 in front of the São Vicente Chapel, the site of the clashes between the gang and the local population.[[207]](#cite_note-207)

The Feast of Saint Lucy is one of the main religious events in Rio Grande do Norte, held in December in front of the cathedral. It begins on 3 December with an opening mass, followed by nine nights of [novenas](/wiki/Novena "Novena"). During the festival, the Saint Lucy Oratory, a theatrical performance depicting the life of [Saint Lucy](/wiki/Saint_Lucy "Saint Lucy"), is staged after the novenas. Other events include the Saint Lucy Cavalcade, the Light Pedalling, the Light Motorcycle Pilgrimage, and musical performances.[[208]](#cite_note-208) The festivities conclude on 13 December with masses and a traditional procession, attracting up to 100,000 devotees from Mossoró and beyond. Through state law 10,114, enacted on 7 October 2016, the festival was recognized as a cultural, historical, and intangible heritage of Rio Grande do Norte.[[209]](#cite_note-209)

Other significant events in Mossoró's cultural calendar include:[[210]](#cite_note-Prefeitura-turismo-210) the Goat Festival, held at the Armando Buá Exhibition Park by the Rio Grande do Norte state government in partnership with the municipal government and local associations. Its program features exhibitions of cattle, goats, sheep, and pigs, as well as artisanal products, a gastronomic festival, artistic performances, and other attractions;[[211]](#cite_note-211)[[212]](#cite_note-212) the Western Region Industrial and Commercial Fair (FICRO), which has been held since 1987 and is organized by the Mossoró Commercial and Industrial Association (ACIM);[[213]](#cite_note-213) the Mossoró Book Fair, a reading promotion event held since 2005, featuring book exhibitions and a diverse program;[[214]](#cite_note-214) the International Fair of Irrigated Tropical Horticulture (Expofruit), the main event for irrigated horticulture in Brazil, with participation from numerous national and international companies;[[215]](#cite_note-215) and the Freedom Act, the main theatrical performance held during the Freedom Festival at the end of September, which commemorates three of Mossoró's four libertarian acts: the Women's Revolt (1875), the abolition of slavery (1883), and the first female vote (1927), alongside parades and musical performances.[[216]](#cite_note-216)[[217]](#cite_note-217)

### Sports

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mossor%C3%B3&action=edit&section=23 "Edit section: Sports")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/9/95/Nogueir%C3%A3o.jpg/250px-Nogueir%C3%A3o.jpg)](/wiki/File:Nogueir%C3%A3o.jpg)

A match between [Baraúnas de Mossoró](/wiki/Associa%C3%A7%C3%A3o_Cultural_Esporte_Clube_Bara%C3%BAnas "Associação Cultural Esporte Clube Baraúnas") and [Campinense](/wiki/Campinense_Clube "Campinense Clube") (from [Campina Grande](/wiki/Campina_Grande "Campina Grande"), [Paraíba](/wiki/Para%C3%ADba "Paraíba")) at the [Nogueirão](/wiki/Nogueir%C3%A3o "Nogueirão") during the [2012 Campeonato Brasileiro Série D](/wiki/2012_Campeonato_Brasileiro_S%C3%A9rie_D "2012 Campeonato Brasileiro Série D"). Baraúnas won 2–0.

The first football club in Mossoró was Humaitá Futebol Clube, founded on 14 October 1919.[[218]](#cite_note-218) The city is home to the [Estádio Leonardo Nogueira](/wiki/Nogueir%C3%A3o "Nogueirão"), popularly known as *Nogueirão*, established on 25 January 1922,[[219]](#cite_note-Estádio-219) which serves as the home stadium for two traditional local football clubs: [Associação Cultural e Desportiva Potiguar](/wiki/Associa%C3%A7%C3%A3o_Cultural_e_Desportiva_Potiguar "Associação Cultural e Desportiva Potiguar") (Potiguar de Mossoró) and [Associação Cultural Esporte Clube Baraúnas](/wiki/Associa%C3%A7%C3%A3o_Cultural_Esporte_Clube_Bara%C3%BAnas "Associação Cultural Esporte Clube Baraúnas") (Baraúnas). The former has won two titles (2004 and [2013](/wiki/2013_Campeonato_Potiguar "2013 Campeonato Potiguar")) and secured three runner-up positions (1997, 2006, and 2008) in the [Campeonato Potiguar](/wiki/Campeonato_Potiguar "Campeonato Potiguar"), while the latter has one title (2006) and two runner-up positions (1981 and 1987).

Several sports events are held annually in Mossoró, including the Governador Dix-Sept Rosado Cycling Race, a cycling race held since 1949 across various categories, making it one of Brazil's oldest [cycling](/wiki/Cycling "Cycling") events. Originally conducted on a route between Governador Dix-Sept Rosado (then a district, now a municipality) and Mossoró, it is now held solely within the city's urban streets.[[220]](#cite_note-prefeitura-esporte-220)[[221]](#cite_note-221) Another notable event is the Municipal School Games (JEM's), which brings together various sports and involves students from multiple schools in the municipality, aiming to promote engagement in sports.[[220]](#cite_note-prefeitura-esporte-220)[[222]](#cite_note-222)

The municipal government also closes the *Cultural Corridor* on Sundays to facilitate sports activities such as [walking](/wiki/Walking "Walking"), cycling, [inline skating](/wiki/Inline_skating "Inline skating"), and [skateboarding](/wiki/Skateboarding "Skateboarding"),[[223]](#cite_note-223) and has adapted several public squares for sports, including the Praça do Inocoop[[224]](#cite_note-224) and the Praça do [Rotary Club](/wiki/Rotary_International "Rotary International").[[225]](#cite_note-225)

The city also features the Engenheiro Pedro Ciarlini Neto Sports Gymnasium ([sports complex](/wiki/Sports_complex "Sports complex")),[[226]](#cite_note-226) the Sports Square (multi-sport square),[[227]](#cite_note-227) and the Skateboard Square,[[228]](#cite_note-228) as well as a [BMX](/wiki/BMX "BMX") track at the Praça Maria de Lourdes Fernandes Moreira in Ulrick Graff,[[229]](#cite_note-229) a [motocross](/wiki/Motocross "Motocross") track in Alto da Pelonha,[[230]](#cite_note-230) and a [go-kart](/wiki/Go-kart "Go-kart") track in the Cidade Nova neighborhood.[[231]](#cite_note-231)[[232]](#cite_note-232) Additionally, various facilities for [seven-a-side football](/wiki/Seven-a-side_football "Seven-a-side football") and [health clubs](/wiki/Health_club "Health club") are spread throughout the city.[[233]](#cite_note-233)[[234]](#cite_note-234)

### Leisure areas

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mossor%C3%B3&action=edit&section=24 "Edit section: Leisure areas")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/9/92/Parque_Municipal_Professor_Maur%C3%ADcio_de_Oliveira.jpg/250px-Parque_Municipal_Professor_Maur%C3%ADcio_de_Oliveira.jpg)](/wiki/File:Parque_Municipal_Professor_Maur%C3%ADcio_de_Oliveira.jpg)

Professor Maurício de Oliveira Municipal Park

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/17/Piscina_do_CEPE_Mossor%C3%B3_%28ASPETRO%29.jpg/250px-Piscina_do_CEPE_Mossor%C3%B3_%28ASPETRO%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:Piscina_do_CEPE_Mossor%C3%B3_(ASPETRO).jpg)

One of the pools at CEPE Mossoró (ASPETRO)

The municipality of Mossoró offers several leisure facilities, including the AABB,[[235]](#cite_note-235) Assec,[[236]](#cite_note-236) CEPE Mossoró (ASPETRO),[[237]](#cite_note-237) Clube da Caixa, Clube da Cosern, Garbos Trade Hotel,[[238]](#cite_note-238) Hotel e Resort Thermas,[[239]](#cite_note-239) Hotel VillaOeste,[[240]](#cite_note-240) Parque Municipal Professor Maurício de Oliveira,[[241]](#cite_note-241) Praça das Crianças,[[242]](#cite_note-242) [SESC](/wiki/Servi%C3%A7o_Social_do_Com%C3%A9rcio "Serviço Social do Comércio"),[[243]](#cite_note-243) [SESI](/wiki/Industrial_Social_Services "Industrial Social Services"),[[244]](#cite_note-244) SEST,[[245]](#cite_note-245) Sindicato dos Bancários,[[246]](#cite_note-246) and SINDPREVS,[[247]](#cite_note-247) as well as numerous [public squares](/wiki/Public_squares "Public squares") scattered throughout the municipality, such as the Praça do Rotary Club. Additionally, every year during the festivities of [Saint Lucy](/wiki/Lucy_of_Syracuse "Lucy of Syracuse") in December, the city hosts an [amusement park](/wiki/Amusement_park "Amusement park"), which in recent years has been set up along the *Cultural Corridor*.[[248]](#cite_note-248)
